message:
feat(robot): add auto-restart-stuck agent detection and config migration e2e test

Two independent additions:

1. Auto-restart stuck agents (internal/robot/health.go, +149 lines):
   - New --robot-health-restart-stuck flag detects agent panes that have been
     idle beyond a configurable threshold (default 5 minutes) and restarts them
   - Pure functions for testability: ClassifyStuckPanes() identifies stuck panes
     from SessionAgentHealth data, BuildAutoRestartStuckOutput() constructs the
     structured JSON response
   - GetAutoRestartStuck() orchestrates: calls GetSessionHealth() to detect idle
     panes, then GetRestartPane() for each stuck pane (skippable with --dry-run)
   - ParseStuckThreshold() handles duration parsing with validation
   - AutoRestartStuckOutput struct includes session, stuck_panes, restarted,
     failed, threshold, dry_run, and checked_at fields
   - CLI wiring: --robot-health-restart-stuck and --stuck-threshold flags in root.go

2. Config migration e2e test (e2e/config_migration_test.go, 181 lines):
   - Tests the v0→v1 config migration path end-to-end
   - Verifies that legacy YAML configs are correctly migrated to the new format

Also syncs beads issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1mlei","title":"Feature: ACFS setup status integration","description":"## Overview
Expose ACFS (flywheel setup) status in NTM to help agents detect missing prerequisites.

## Robot Mode API

```
ntm --robot-acfs-status
```

**Naming Pattern**: Follows tool bridge convention `--robot-<tool>-<action>`

Note: Also aliased as `ntm --robot-setup` for discoverability.

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "acfs_version": "1.2.3",
  "tools": {
    "br": {"installed": true, "version": "0.8.0", "path": "/usr/local/bin/br"},
    "cc": {"installed": true, "version": "1.0.0", "path": "/usr/local/bin/claude"},
    "cod": {"installed": false, "hint": "npm install -g @openai/codex-cli"}
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- NTM can report ACFS presence and version
- Shows which core tools are installed with versions
- Missing tools surface actionable hints","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

Comprehensive unit tests for the Ollama/local LLM integration.

## Test Coverage Required

### 1. Ollama Adapter Tests (ollama_adapter_test.go)
- TestConnect: Successfully connects to Ollama server
- TestConnect_Unavailable: Handles connection failure gracefully
- TestSendPrompt: Sends prompt and receives response
- TestSendPrompt_Timeout: Handles timeout correctly
- TestStreamResponse: Streaming tokens work
- TestStreamResponse_Cancel: Context cancellation works
- TestListModels: Returns available models
- TestPullModel: Initiates model download
- TestPullModel_Progress: Progress callbacks work

### 2. Mock Ollama Server
```go
type MockOllamaServer struct {
    models    []string
    responses map[string]string
    latency   time.Duration
}

func (m *MockOllamaServer) HandleGenerate(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    // Simulate streaming response
}
```

### 3. Spawn Integration Tests (spawn_local_test.go)
- TestSpawnLocal_FlagParsing: --local=N parsed correctly
- TestSpawnLocal_ModelFlag: --local-model=X works
- TestSpawnLocal_HostFlag: --local-host=X works
- TestSpawnLocal_MixedFleet: --cc=2 --local=2 works
- TestSpawnLocal_Validation: Invalid model handled
- TestSpawnLocal_OllamaDown: Graceful error when unavailable

### 4. Performance Metrics Tests (metrics_test.go)
- TestTokensPerSecond: Calculation correct
- TestTokensPerSecond_Window: Sliding window works
- TestMemoryUsage: Parses Ollama /api/ps response
- TestLatency: First-token latency tracked
- TestMetricsAggregation: Metrics combine correctly

### 5. Model Management Tests (models_test.go)
- TestListModels: Lists cached models
- TestListModels_Empty: Handles no models
- TestPullModel_Success: Downloads model
- TestPullModel_AlreadyExists: Skips existing
- TestRemoveModel: Deletes model
- TestRecommendModel: Suggests based on VRAM
- TestRecommendModel_NoGPU: CPU-only recommendations

## Test Fixtures
```go
var mockOllamaResponse = `{"model":"codellama:7b","created_at":"2025-01-15T10:00:00Z","response":"func main() {","done":false}
{"model":"codellama:7b","created_at":"2025-01-15T10:00:00Z","response":"\n","done":false}
{"model":"codellama:7b","created_at":"2025-01-15T10:00:00Z","response":"","done":true,"total_duration":1234567890}`
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- 85%+ code coverage
- Mock server enables offline testing
- All tests pass without real Ollama
- Tests run in <10 seconds","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:21:20.579646406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:27:07.571527098Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-1t8p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-1tb8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-852e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-a7wm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mnb9","depends_on_id":"bd-zcft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1mxyl","title":"DCG: Add dashboard indicator for DCG status","description":"Add visual indicator in NTM dashboard showing DCG protection status.

## Design

### Location
Top-right status area of dashboard, alongside other status indicators.

### Visual States
- 🛡️ DCG (green): DCG active and protecting
- ⚠️ DCG (yellow): DCG enabled but binary not found
- (none): DCG disabled in config

### Tooltip/Details
When selected, show:
- Commands checked this session
- Commands blocked this session
- Last blocked command (if any)

## Implementation

### Dashboard Model Update
```go
type DashboardModel struct {
    // ... existing fields
    DCGStatus DCGStatusModel
}

type DCGStatusModel struct {
    Enabled   bool
    Available bool
    Checked   int64
    Blocked   int64
    LastBlock *BlockedCommand
}
```

### View Update
Add to dashboard header/status bar:
```go
func (m DashboardModel) View() string {
    // ... existing view code
    dcgIndicator := m.renderDCGIndicator()
    // Insert into status bar
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dcg_indicator.go
- Update: internal/tui/dashboard/model.go

## Styling
Use lipgloss for consistent styling with rest of dashboard","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:23.254864594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:20:40.472504211Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:20:40.472457132Z","close_reason":"Implemented DCG status indicator badge in dashboard stats bar","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mxyl","depends_on_id":"bd-1m6sw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mxyl","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9px","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mxyl","depends_on_id":"bd-o97j9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1my2","title":"Task: Implement agent capability matrix","description":"## Purpose
Define capability scores per agent type x task type for intelligent assignment.

## Capability Matrix
var agentCapabilities = map[AgentType]map[string]float64{
    AgentClaude: {
        'refactor': 0.95,
        'analysis': 0.90,
        'docs':     0.85,
        'bug':      0.80,
        'feature':  0.85,
        'testing':  0.75,
    },
    AgentCodex: {
        'feature':  0.90,
        'bug':      0.90,
        'testing':  0.85,
        'refactor': 0.75,
        'docs':     0.70,
    },
    AgentGemini: {
        'docs':     0.90,
        'analysis': 0.85,
        'feature':  0.80,
        'testing':  0.80,
    },
}

## Usage
func GetAgentScore(agentType AgentType, taskType string) float64 {
    if learned, ok := learnedScores[agentType][taskType]; ok {
        return learned  // Use learned scores if available
    }
    return agentCapabilities[agentType][taskType]
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default scores for all agent/task combinations
- Scores between 0.0 and 1.0
- Configurable overrides
- Integrates with learned scores (Epic #15)

## Technical Notes
Store in internal/assign/capability.go
Allow config overrides","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:43:13.131776968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:08:41.161147162Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:08:41.161100564Z","close_reason":"Implemented agent capability matrix in internal/assign/capability.go with tests. Features: default scores, configurable overrides, learned scores support, thread-safe access.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1my2","depends_on_id":"bd-9tbv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1syw","title":"Task: Register agents with Agent Mail on spawn","description":"## Purpose
Automatically register each spawned agent with Agent Mail for inter-agent communication.

## Scope
- On spawn, call register_agent for each non-user pane
- Use auto-generated adjective+noun names per Agent Mail conventions
- Store pane -> agent name mapping for session
- Create project-wide coordination thread

## Registration Flow
for _, pane := range panes {
    if pane.Type == AgentUser { continue }
    
    agent, err := am.RegisterAgent(ctx, RegisterAgentOptions{
        ProjectKey:      s.projectDir,
        Program:         'ntm',
        Model:           pane.Variant,
        TaskDescription: fmt.Sprintf('Agent in pane %s', pane.Title),
    })
    
    s.agentRegistry[pane.ID] = agent.Name
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents registered with unique names
- Mapping stored for message routing
- Graceful handling when Agent Mail unavailable
- Registration survives session restart

## Technical Notes
Reuse existing agentmail client
Store mapping in session state","notes":"## Testing Requirements

- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1t881","title":"Test: Unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching against actual agent output, covering all rate limit variations.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/limit_detector_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "testing"
    "time"
)

func TestLimitDetector_PatternMatching(t *testing.T) {
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    
    // Test all known rate limit patterns
    patterns := []struct {
        name       string
        agentType  string
        output     string
        shouldMatch bool
        matchType   string
    }{
        // Claude Code patterns
        {
            name:       "claude_rate_limit_standard",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Error: rate limit exceeded. Please wait before trying again.",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_429_error",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "API Error 429: Too Many Requests",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "429",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_token_limit",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "You have exceeded your token limit for this billing period",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "token_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_normal_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "I'll help you implement that function. Let me start by...",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        
        // Codex patterns
        {
            name:       "codex_rate_limit",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "openai.RateLimitError: Rate limit reached for default-gpt-4",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "codex_quota_exceeded",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Gemini patterns  
        {
            name:       "gemini_resource_exhausted",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "google.api_core.exceptions.ResourceExhausted: 429 Resource exhausted",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "resource_exhausted",
        },
        {
            name:       "gemini_quota_limit",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "Quota exceeded for GenerateContent",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Edge cases
        {
            name:       "partial_match",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "The word rate appears but limit does not follow immediately",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        {
            name:       "case_insensitive",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "RATE LIMIT EXCEEDED",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "multiline_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Processing...\nWorking...\nError: rate limit exceeded\nRetrying...",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "empty_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range patterns {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Agent: %s", tt.agentType)
            t.Logf("Output: %q", truncateForLog(tt.output, 100))
            t.Logf("Expected match: %v", tt.shouldMatch)
            
            match, pattern := detector.CheckOutput(tt.agentType, tt.output)
            
            t.Logf("Result: match=%v, pattern=%q", match, pattern)
            
            if tt.shouldMatch {
                assert.True(t, match, "Should detect rate limit")
                assert.NotEmpty(t, pattern, "Should identify pattern type")
                if tt.matchType != "" {
                    assert.Contains(t, pattern, tt.matchType, "Pattern type mismatch")
                }
            } else {
                assert.False(t, match, "Should NOT detect rate limit")
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_CapturePane(t *testing.T) {
    // Create mock tmux client
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{
        captureResponses: map[string]string{
            "cc_agents_1:1.0": "Normal agent output here",
            "cc_agents_1:1.1": "Error: rate limit exceeded",
            "cc_agents_1:1.2": "", // Empty pane
        },
    }
    
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    
    tests := []struct {
        paneID      string
        expectLimit bool
    }{
        {"cc_agents_1:1.0", false},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.1", true},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.2", false},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.paneID, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Capturing pane: %s", tt.paneID)
            
            output, err := detector.CaptureAndCheck(tt.paneID, "cc")
            require.NoError(t, err)
            
            t.Logf("Captured output length: %d", len(output))
            t.Logf("Limit detected: %v", tt.expectLimit)
            
            match, _ := detector.CheckOutput("cc", output)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expectLimit, match)
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_BackgroundMonitoring(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{}
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    detector.pollInterval = 50 * time.Millisecond
    
    t.Log("Starting background monitoring")
    
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 200*time.Millisecond)
    defer cancel()
    
    eventCh := detector.Events()
    
    // Start monitoring
    go detector.StartMonitoring(ctx, []PaneSpec{
        {Index: 1, AgentType: "cc"},
    }, "test_session")
    
    // Inject rate limit after short delay
    go func() {
        time.Sleep(75 * time.Millisecond)
        mockTmux.SetCaptureResponse("test_session:1.1", "rate limit exceeded")
    }()
    
    // Wait for event
    select {
    case event := <-eventCh:
        t.Logf("Event received: %+v", event)
        assert.Equal(t, "cc", event.AgentType)
        assert.Contains(t, event.Pattern, "rate")
    case <-ctx.Done():
        t.Log("Context cancelled before event (expected if timing varies)")
    }
}

// Helpers
func truncateForLog(s string, max int) string {
    if len(s) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return s[:max] + "..."
}
```

- Log pattern type when matched
- Log pane capture details
- Log background monitoring events

- [ ] Test all known Codex limit patterns

- [ ] Test multiline output
- [ ] Test empty output
- [ ] Test partial/false matches
- [ ] Test pane capture integration
- [ ] Test background monitoring
- [ ] Mock tmux client for isolation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:54.929888923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:57:04.608090689Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:57:04.608007314Z","close_reason":"Added LimitDetector tests (all patterns, multiline, capture, monitoring); go test ./internal/swarm -run LimitDetector","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1t881","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1t881","depends_on_id":"bd-3un3t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1t8p","title":"EPIC: Local LLM Support via Ollama and llama.cpp","description":"## Overview
Add support for local LLM backends (Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio) as first-class agent types, enabling privacy-sensitive work, offline operation, and cost-free experimentation.

## Problem Statement
Current NTM only supports cloud LLM providers (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini). Users need:
- Privacy for sensitive codebases (air-gapped environments)
- Offline operation when internet unavailable
- Cost-free experimentation and learning
- Running specialized/fine-tuned local models

## Key Features
1. New agent type: --local=N or --ollama=N for spawning local agents
2. Model selection: --local-model=codellama:34b
3. Mixed fleets: combine cloud and local agents in same session
4. Performance monitoring: track token/s, memory usage
5. Automatic model management: pull models on demand

## Technical Design
1. Ollama adapter:
   - Connect to local Ollama server (default: localhost:11434)
   - Map NTM commands to Ollama API
   - Handle streaming responses

2. llama.cpp adapter:
   - Direct integration for llama.cpp server mode
   - Support GGUF model loading
   - GPU acceleration detection

3. Agent type hierarchy:
   - CloudAgent (existing): Claude, Codex, Gemini
   - LocalAgent (new): Ollama, LlamaCpp
   - All agents implement same interface

## Configuration
- NTM_OLLAMA_HOST for custom Ollama URL
- NTM_LOCAL_MODEL for default model
- Per-project config in ntm.yaml

## Success Criteria
- Can spawn local agents alongside cloud agents
- Local agents participate in normal workflows
- Performance metrics visible in dashboard
- Works fully offline (after model cached)

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/agent/local.go (new - local agent implementation)
- internal/agent/ollama.go (new - Ollama adapter)
- internal/cli/spawn.go (add --local flags)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/agents.go (show local agent metrics)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:29:25.999766320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:48:22.570133773Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-1tb8","title":"Task: Add automatic model management for local LLMs","description":"## Overview
Enable NTM to automatically pull and manage local LLM models when needed.

## Deliverables
1. Model management commands:
   - ntm models list - show available local models
   - ntm models pull <model> - download model from Ollama registry
   - ntm models remove <model> - delete model to free space
   - ntm models recommend - suggest models based on hardware

2. Automatic pull on spawn:
   - If --local-model not found locally, offer to pull
   - Show download progress in terminal
   - Continue spawn after model ready

3. Model recommendations:
   Based on available VRAM:
   - <4GB: codellama:7b-instruct, deepseek-coder:6.7b
   - 4-8GB: codellama:13b, deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct
   - 8-16GB: codellama:34b, deepseek-coder:33b
   - 16GB+: mixtral:8x7b, codellama:70b

4. Storage management:
   - Show model sizes and disk usage
   - Warn when disk space low
   - LRU cleanup suggestion for unused models

## Implementation Notes
- Use Ollama API for all model operations
- Cache model metadata locally for fast lookup
- Support offline operation with cached models

## Acceptance Criteria
- Model commands work correctly
- Automatic pull flows smoothly
- Recommendations match hardware
- Disk space warnings appear appropriately
- Unit tests for model management","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureMoose","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:30:23.066483753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:44:40.397765934Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tb8","depends_on_id":"bd-1t8p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tb8","depends_on_id":"bd-zcft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1tmw5","title":"CAAM: Add --robot-switch-account command","description":"Add robot mode command to manually trigger account switch.

## Command Specification

```bash
ntm --robot-switch-account claude         # Switch to next Claude account
ntm --robot-switch-account openai account3  # Switch to specific account
ntm --robot-switch-account --pane agent-1 claude  # Switch for specific pane
```

## Output Schema (JSON)

```json
{
  "switch": {
    "success": true,
    "provider": "claude",
    "previous_account": "account1@email.com",
    "new_account": "account2@email.com",
    "panes_affected": ["agent-1", "agent-2"],
    "cooldown_seconds": 300
  }
}
```

## Implementation
1. Parse provider and optional account name
2. Call CAAM switch
3. Update affected pane environments
4. Return result JSON

## Code Location
- internal/robot/switch_account.go
- Update: internal/robot/registry.go

## Testing
- Switch to next account, verify output
- Switch to specific account
- Switch with pane filter
- Switch when no accounts available","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:52:03.353964001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:22:05.289374984Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:22:05.289301996Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-switch-account command for CAAM account switching.  {"id":"bd-kjihq","title":"Swarm CLI: Add --output flag for plan JSON export","description":"## Purpose
Add `--output` flag to the `ntm swarm` subcommand for writing the generated plan to a JSON file.

**NOTE**: This is a SUBCOMMAND flag for `ntm swarm`, NOT a top-level robot flag.

## Command Interface

```bash
# Subcommand usage (NOT robot mode)
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/projects --output=plan.json
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/projects --dry-run --output=plan.json

# Robot mode for querying swarm state is separate (see bd-3cdsi)
ntm --robot-snapshot  # Includes swarm state in unified output
```

## Implementation Details

```go
// In cmd/ntm/swarm.go - subcommand flag, not root robot flag
var outputPath string

func init() {
    swarmCmd.Flags().StringVar(&outputPath, "output", "",
        "Write swarm plan to JSON file (optional)")
}

func runSwarm(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // ... generate plan ...
    
    if outputPath != "" {
        if err := writePlanToFile(plan, outputPath); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("write plan: %w", err)
        }
        slog.Info("plan written", "path", outputPath)
    }
    return nil
}

func writePlanToFile(plan *swarm.SwarmPlan, path string) error {
    dir := filepath.Dir(path)
    if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("create directory: %w", err)
    }
    
    data, err := json.MarshalIndent(plan, "", "  ")
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("marshal plan: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Atomic write
    tmpPath := path + ".tmp"
    if err := os.WriteFile(tmpPath, data, 0644); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    return os.Rename(tmpPath, path)
}
```

## Output Format
```json
{
  "created_at": "2026-01-22T10:30:00Z",
  "scan_dir": "/home/user/projects",
  "allocations": [...],
  "total_cc": 10,
  "total_cod": 8,
  "total_gmi": 6,
  "sessions": [...]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flag is on swarm subcommand (not root)
- [ ] Works with --dry-run
- [ ] Atomic write via temp file
- [ ] Parent directory auto-creation
- [ ] Clear error messages","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletEagle","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:27.275998902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T22:09:42.468830388Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T22:09:42.468762612Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: ntm swarm --output writes SwarmPlan JSON (atomic) + unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kjihq","depends_on_id":"bd-2pgo6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-kjihq","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-kk81t","title":"Implement recommendation generator","description":"Implement recommendation generator for diagnosed issues.

## Recommendations per Status

### rate_limited
- action: wait_or_caam
- reason: 'Output contains rate limit pattern'
- Parse reset_time if available
- auto_fixable: false (requires human decision)

### unresponsive
- action: kill_restart
- reason: 'No output for N seconds, process state: X'
- auto_fixable: true
- fix_command: 'ntm --robot-restart-pane <session> <pane>'

### crashed
- action: restart_cc
- reason: 'No agent process' or 'Zombie process'
- auto_fixable: true
- fix_command: 'ntm --robot-restart-pane <session> <pane>'

## Output Format

Each recommendation includes:
- pane: int
- status: string
- action: string
- reason: string (human readable)
- auto_fixable: bool
- fix_command: string (if auto_fixable)
- Additional context (reset_time, etc.)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:27:33.219751841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.657219571Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.657174857Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-31e1f diagnose.go - includes --diagnose-pane flag, overall health classification, --diagnose-fix auto-remediation, recommendation generator, and health classification engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kk81t","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"bd-krqz","title":"Add ntm health --auto-restart-stuck - detect and restart agents that appear stuck (no output for N minutes)","description":"Add ntm health --auto-restart-stuck - detect and restart agents that appear stuck (no output for N minutes).

## Problem Being Solved

Agents sometimes hang or get stuck. Currently requires manual detection and restart.

## Command Design

```bash
ntm health SESSION --auto-restart-stuck [--threshold DURATION] [--dry-run]
```

### Behavior
1. Monitors all agent panes for output activity
2. Detects panes with no output for threshold duration
3. Automatically restarts stuck panes

### Detection Logic
- Track last output timestamp per pane
- Pane is 'stuck' if no output for threshold (default: 5m)
- Ignore panes that are intentionally idle (awaiting user input)

### Options
- --threshold: Duration before considering stuck (default: 5m)
- --dry-run: Report stuck panes without restarting

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-health-restart-stuck=SESSION [--threshold=DURATION]

Returns JSON:
```json
{
  "session": "my_session",
  "stuck_panes": [3, 5],
  "restarted": [3, 5],
  "threshold": "5m"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. --auto-restart-stuck flag added to ntm health command
2. Detects panes with no output for threshold duration
3. Default threshold is 5 minutes
4. --threshold flag allows custom duration
5. --dry-run reports without restarting
6. Restart uses existing --robot-restart-pane logic
7. Robot mode returns JSON with stuck/restarted panes

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/health_test.go
- Test stuck detection logic
- Test threshold parsing

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-AUTO-RESTART] Auto-restart Stuck Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-AUTO-RESTART]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing --auto-restart-stuck"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. ntm health --auto-restart-stuck detects stuck agents
2. No output for N minutes triggers detection
3. Configurable threshold (default 5 minutes)
4. Automatic restart with confirmation option
5. Logging of restart events
6. Unit tests for detection logic
7. E2E test with simulated stuck agent","notes":"## Testing Requirements

 {"id":"bd-ktyql","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Env Info","description":"E2E tests for Robot Env Info with actual sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Info Retrieval
Spawn session with --cc=3, verify tmux.binary_path, tmux.version, window_index, agent_pane_start

### Scenario 2: Structure Accuracy
Spawn session with 5 agents, verify total_agent_panes, agent_pane_end, control_pane

### Scenario 3: Targeting Examples Work
Get --robot-env output, parse example_agent_pane, use target to send keys, verify delivery

### Scenario 4: Shell Detection
Verify shell.type detection (bash/zsh), check tmux_plugin_detected

### Scenario 5: Timing Values
Verify all timing values present and reasonable (100ms for ctrl_c_gap, etc.)

### Scenario 6: Config Override
Set custom timing in config, verify custom values reflected in --robot-env

## Logging Requirements
Each test logs: test name, spawn_args, full env_response JSON, verification outputs

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 6 test scenarios implemented as bash scripts
2. Each scenario logs full --robot-env JSON response
3. Tests verify structure matches expected schema
4. Shell detection works for bash and zsh
5. Timing values are within reasonable ranges
6. Config overrides properly reflected
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. All 6 test scenarios pass
2. Test logs follow JSON format specification
3. Cleanup removes all test sessions
4. Tests are idempotent (can run multiple times)
5. Test duration under 3 minutes total
6. Each scenario logs setup/execute/verify/result
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, 1 on any failure
8.
